content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 16, 1991


Debra M. Hight, Deputy City Attorney
City of Fresno City Attorney's Office
City Hall
Fresno, CA 93721

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of
Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-91-174

Dear Ms. Hight:

By letter dated March 8, 1991, you are seeking confirmation
of telephone advice provided to you by this office on March 6,
1991. You had inquired whether as the legal advisor for the
city's planning commission, you had a disqualifying interest under
the Political Reform Act (the Act) in planning commission
decision approving a conditional use permit for a water well
located approximately 1500 feet from your residence. While your
letter generally summarizes the advice you were provided, some
clarification is necessary.

You indicated in your telephone inquiry that the conditional
use permit was for a new well that would supplement an existing
well currently providing water service to your residence. You
were advised that a potentially disqualifying conflict might exist
if as a result of the decision concerning the conditional use
permit, (1) the fair market value of your residence was impacted,
positively or negatively, by $10,000 or more (Regulation
18702.3(a)(3)(A)); or (2) your property would receive new or
substantially improved services (Regulation 18702.3(a)(2)).

You indicated that whatever impact on your residence's fair
market value occurred as a consequence of the decision on the
conditional use permit was at best speculative. You also
indicated that it was similarly speculative whether, as a result
of the decision, the water service to your residence would be


1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. I-91-174
Page 2

"substantially improved," since (a) the new well would supplement,
and not replace, the existing well service, and (b) it was not
known whether the water from the new well would be of a higher
quality than that provided by the existing well. In rendering
advice, the Commission does not act as a finder of fact.
Therefore, on the basis of your representations, you were advised
that it did not appear that a disqualifying conflict was present.

I trust this letter provides you with the confirmation you
have requested. Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you have
any further questions regarding this matter.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:ken

sections.question: Whether as the legal advisor for the city's planning commission, you had a disqualifying interest under the Political Reform Act in a planning commission decision concerning a conditional use permit for a water well located approximately 1500 feet from your residence.
sections.conclusion: On the basis of your representations, you were advised that it did not appear that a disqualifying conflict was present, given that: (1) any impact on the fair market value of the residence was speculative; and (2) whether the water service would be substantially improved was similarly speculative since the new well would supplement rather than replace the existing well and it was unknown whether the water quality would be higher.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a city attorney serving as legal advisor to the planning commission have a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when voting on a conditional use permit for a water well located 1500 feet from her residence?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that no disqualifying conflict appears to exist based on the requestor's representations that impacts on property value and service improvements were speculative rather than concrete.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is confirmation of prior telephone advice. Question and conclusion sections identified and extracted verbatim. Document dated April 16, 1991, File No. I-91-174. Clear structure with advice letter format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether as the legal advisor for the city's planning commission, you had a disqualifying interest under the Political Reform Act in a planning commission decision concerning a conditional use permit for a water well located approximately 1500 feet from your residence.

CONCLUSION: On the basis of your representations, you were advised that it did not appear that a disqualifying conflict was present, given that: (1) any impact on the fair market value of the residence was speculative; and (2) whether the water service would be substantially improved was similarly speculative since the new well would supplement rather than replace the existing well and it was unknown whether the water quality would be higher.

You had inquired whether as the legal advisor for the city's planning commission, you had a disqualifying interest under the Political Reform Act (the Act) in planning commission decision approving a conditional use permit for a water well located approximately 1500 feet from your residence. While your letter generally summarizes the advice you were provided, some clarification is necessary. You indicated in your telephone inquiry that the conditional use permit was for a new well that would supplement an existing well currently providing water service to your residence. You were advised that a potentially disqualifying conflict might exist if as a result of the decision concerning the conditional use permit, (1) the fair market value of your residence was impacted, positively or negatively, by $10,000 or more (Regulation 18702.3(a)(3)(A)); or (2) your property would receive new or substantially improved services (Regulation 18702.3(a)(2)). You indicated that whatever impact on your residence's fair market value occurred as a consequence of the decision on the conditional use permit was at best speculative. embedding.summary: This advice letter confirms telephone guidance provided to the Deputy City Attorney of Fresno regarding potential disqualifying conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act concerning a planning commission decision on a water well conditional use permit near her residence. The FPPC concluded no disqualifying conflict was present based on the speculative nature of impacts to property value and service improvements.
